---
title : Figure 14.4
subtitle : Comparison of LDA with a full covariance matrix, LDA with a diagonal covariance matrix, and logistic regression
author : Thimoté Dupuch
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: zephyr
      dark: darkly
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg; Pkg.activate("../.."); Pkg.status()
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
using CairoMakie, Statistics, Random, LinearAlgebra
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
```

```{julia}
Random.seed!(1)

function fit_lda(X1, X0)
    n1, d = size(X1)
    n0 = size(X0, 1)
    n = n1 + n0
    mu1 = vec(mean(X1, dims=1))
    mu0 = vec(mean(X0, dims=1))
    Sigma1 = (X1 .- mu1')' * (X1 .- mu1') / n1
    Sigma0 = (X0 .- mu0')' * (X0 .- mu0') / n0
    Sigma = (n0/n) * Sigma0 + (n1/n) * Sigma1
    w = Sigma \ (mu1 - mu0)
    b = -0.5 * dot(mu1 + mu0, w) + log(n1 / n0)
    return w, b
end

function fit_lda_diag(X1, X0)
    n1, d = size(X1)
    n0 = size(X0, 1)
    n = n1 + n0
    mu1 = vec(mean(X1, dims=1))
    mu0 = vec(mean(X0, dims=1))
    Sigma1 = (X1 .- mu1')' * (X1 .- mu1') / n1
    Sigma0 = (X0 .- mu0')' * (X0 .- mu0') / n0
    Sigma = Diagonal(diag((n0/n) * Sigma0 + (n1/n) * Sigma1))
    w = Sigma \ (mu1 - mu0)
    b = -0.5 * dot(mu1 + mu0, w) + log(n1 / n0)
    return w, b
end

function fit_logistic(X, y; λ=1e-12, max_iter=100, tol=1e-10)
    n, d = size(X)
    Xint = hcat(X, ones(n))
    theta = zeros(d + 1)
    for _ in 1:max_iter
        eta = Xint * theta
        mu = 1.0 ./ (1.0 .+ exp.(-eta))
        gradient = Xint' * (y .- mu) .- λ .* theta
        W = mu .* (1.0 .- mu)
        H = -Xint' * (Xint .* W) - λ * I
        theta = theta - H \ gradient
        any(isnan, theta) && break
        norm(gradient) < tol && break
    end
    return theta[1:d], theta[d+1]
end

compute_error(X, y, w, b) = mean(abs.((sign.(X * w .+ b) .+ 1) ./ 2 .- y))

function run_experiment(n1, n0, d, ts, nrep)
    nts = length(ts)
    err_lda = zeros(nrep, nts)
    err_lda_diag = zeros(nrep, nts)
    err_logistic = zeros(nrep, nts)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        mu1ast = randn(d) * (2 / sqrt(d))
        mu0ast = randn(d) * (2 / sqrt(d))
        noise1_train = randn(n1, d)
        noise0_train = randn(n0, d)
        noise1_test  = randn(n1, d)
        noise0_test  = randn(n0, d)

        for (it, t) in enumerate(ts)
            Sigma_sqrt1 = exp(-t * randn(d, d) / 8)
            Sigma_sqrt0 = exp(-t * randn(d, d) / 8)

            X1 = mu1ast' .+ noise1_train * Sigma_sqrt1
            X0 = mu0ast' .+ noise0_train * Sigma_sqrt0
            X = vcat(X1, X0)
            y = vcat(ones(n1), zeros(n0))

            X1_test = mu1ast' .+ noise1_test * Sigma_sqrt1
            X0_test = mu0ast' .+ noise0_test * Sigma_sqrt0
            X_test = vcat(X1_test, X0_test)
            y_test = vcat(ones(n1), zeros(n0))

            w, b = fit_lda(X1, X0)
            err_lda[irep, it] = compute_error(X_test, y_test, w, b)

            w, b = fit_lda_diag(X1, X0)
            err_lda_diag[irep, it] = compute_error(X_test, y_test, w, b)

            w, b = fit_logistic(X, y)
            err_logistic[irep, it] = compute_error(X_test, y_test, w, b)
        end
    end

    return map(
        x -> vec(mean(x, dims=1)),
        (err_lda, err_lda_diag, err_logistic)
    )

end

ts = 0:0.1:1
d = 50

Random.seed!(2)
err_lda_1, err_diag_1, err_log_1 = run_experiment(50, 50, d, ts, 100)

Random.seed!(2)
err_lda_2, err_diag_2, err_log_2 = run_experiment(500, 500, d, ts, 50)

fig = Figure(size=(800, 400))

ax1 = Axis(
    fig[1, 1], title=L"d = 50, \ n = 50",
    xlabel="lack of independence", ylabel="Error rate", limits=(0, 1, 0, 0.23))
lines!(ax1, ts, err_diag_1, linewidth=2, color=red, label="LDA Diagonal")
lines!(ax1, ts, err_lda_1, linewidth=2, color=blue, label="LDA")
lines!(ax1, ts, err_log_1, linewidth=2, color=:black, label="logistic")
axislegend(ax1, position=:lb)

ax2 = Axis(
    fig[1, 2], title=L"d = 50, \ n = 500",
    xlabel="lack of independence", ylabel="Error rate", limits=(0, 1, 0, 0.23))
lines!(ax2, ts, err_diag_2, linewidth=2, color=red, label="LDA Diagonal")
lines!(ax2, ts, err_lda_2, linewidth=2, color=blue, label="LDA")
lines!(ax2, ts, err_log_2, linewidth=2, color=:black, label="logistic")
axislegend(ax2, position=:lb)

fig

```
